text: The number of ways to arrange the letters of MISSISSIPPI is:
explanation: MISSISSIPPI has M:1, I:4, S:4, P:2. Arrangements \( = \frac{11!}{4!4!2!} \)
name: Repeated Letters Arrangement
subject: maths

text: The time of flight of a projectile launched with speed \(u\) at angle \(\theta\) is:
explanation: Time of flight: \( T = \frac{2u\sin\theta}{g} \). At maximum height, vertical velocity = 0, then returns.
name: Time of Flight
subject: phy

text: The time period of a satellite orbiting at height \(h\) is:
explanation: \( T = \frac{2\pi r}{v_o} = \frac{2\pi r}{\sqrt{GM/r}} = 2\pi\sqrt{\frac{r^3}{GM}} \). This is Kepler's 3rd law.
name: Orbital Period
subject: phy

text: The reaction of HCHO (formaldehyde) with excess NaOH is:
explanation: HCHO has no α-H → Cannizzaro: 2HCHO + NaOH → CH₃OH (methanol) + HCOONa (sodium formate). Cross-Cannizzaro also possible.
name: HCHO Cannizzaro
subject: che

text: The second derivative of \( f(x) = x^3 \) is:
name: Second Derivative
subject: maths

text: The tangent to parabola \( y^2=4ax \) at point \((at^2, 2at)\) is:
explanation: Tangent at parametric point \((at^2,2at)\) on \(y^2=4ax\): \( ty = x + at^2 \)
name: Parametric Tangent to Parabola
subject: maths

text: The magnetic field at the centre of a circular loop of radius \(R\) carrying current \(I\) is:
explanation: Centre of circular loop: \( B = \frac{\mu_0 I}{2R} \). For N turns: \( B = \frac{\mu_0 NI}{2R} \).
name: Field at Centre of Circular Loop
subject: phy

explanation: At \(x=3\): \( 6-1 = B(4) \Rightarrow B = \frac{5}{4} \)
name: Coefficient B
subject: maths

text: The boiling point order among isomeric alkyl halides (same molecular formula) follows:
options: n > iso > neo (more branching = lower bp)
explanation: More branching → more compact molecule → weaker van der Waals forces → lower boiling point. n-alkyl > iso > neo.
name: BP Order of Alkyl Halides
subject: che

text: The difference of focal distances from any point on hyperbola \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \) is:
explanation: For any point on hyperbola: \( |PF_1 - PF_2| = 2a \) (constant). This defines the hyperbola.
name: Focal Distance Difference
subject: maths

text: Which molecule has zero dipole moment despite polar bonds?
explanation: \(\text{CO}_2\) is linear; two C=O dipoles cancel → net dipole = 0. \(\text{H}_2\text{O}\) and \(\text{NH}_3\) have net dipole.
name: Zero Dipole Moment
subject: che

text: The moment of inertia of a ring about its diameter is:
explanation: Ring (axis through centre perpendicular): \( I = MR^2 \). Ring (diameter): \( I = \frac{MR^2}{2} \) (by perpendicular axis theorem: \( MR^2 = I_x + I_y = 2I_{diameter} \)).
name: MOI of Ring about Diameter
subject: phy

text: The mirror formula is:
explanation: Mirror formula: \( \frac{1}{v} + \frac{1}{u} = \frac{1}{f} = \frac{2}{R} \). Sign convention: distances measured from pole; positive in direction of incident light.
name: Mirror Formula
subject: phy

text: The coordinates of a point in the third quadrant satisfy:
explanation: Third quadrant: both \(x\) and \(y\) are negative (\(x<0, y<0\)).
name: Quadrant Sign Convention
subject: maths

text: \( \cos\theta + i\sin\theta \) can also be written as:
explanation: By Euler's formula: \( e^{i\theta} = \cos\theta + i\sin\theta \)
name: Euler's Formula
subject: maths

text: The inverse of \( A = \begin{pmatrix} 2 & 1 \\ 5 & 3 \end{pmatrix} \) is:
explanation: \( |A| = 6-5=1 \). \( A^{-1} = \frac{1}{|A|}\begin{pmatrix}d & -b \\ -c & a\end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix}3 & -1 \\ -5 & 2\end{pmatrix} \)
name: 2x2 Inverse
subject: maths

text: Reduction of carboxylic acids to primary alcohols requires:
explanation: RCOOH → RCH₂OH requires LiAlH₄ (strong reducing agent). NaBH₄ does NOT reduce COOH group. LiAlH₄ reduces COOH to 1° alcohol.
name: Reduction of Carboxylic Acid
subject: che

text: The condition for roots of \( ax^2+bx+c=0 \) to be reciprocal of each other is:
explanation: If roots are \( \alpha, \frac{1}{\alpha} \), product \( = \frac{c}{a} = 1 \Rightarrow a = c \)
name: Reciprocal Roots Condition
subject: maths

text: If rows of a determinant are interchanged, its value:
explanation: Interchanging any two rows (or columns) of a determinant changes its sign.
name: Row Interchange Property
subject: maths

text: Two lines with slopes \( m_1 \) and \( m_2 \) are perpendicular if:
explanation: Lines are perpendicular when \( m_1 \cdot m_2 = -1 \).
name: Perpendicular Lines
subject: maths

text: The energy stored in an inductor with inductance \(L\) carrying current \(I\) is:
explanation: Energy in inductor: \( U = \frac{1}{2}LI^2 \). Stored in magnetic field. Analogous to \( \frac{1}{2}CV^2 \) in capacitor.
name: Energy in Inductor
subject: phy

text: Electrophilic substitution (EAS) on benzene: the rate-determining step involves:
options: Loss of H⁺ from sigma complex
Addition of electrophile to all carbons
explanation: EAS mechanism: (1) Formation of π complex, (2) Formation of σ complex (arenium ion, slow, RDS), (3) Loss of H⁺ (fast) to restore aromaticity.
name: EAS Rate-Determining Step
subject: che

text: Wien's displacement law relates the peak wavelength \(\lambda_m\) to temperature as:
explanation: Wien's law: \( \lambda_m T = b = 2.898\times10^{-3}\,\text{m K} \). Higher temperature → peak at shorter wavelength (blue shift).
name: Wien's Displacement Law
subject: phy

text: In the expansion of \( (a+b)^n \), if \( n = 9 \), then how many middle terms are there?
explanation: When \(n\) is odd, there are 2 middle terms: \( T_{\frac{n+1}{2}} \) and \( T_{\frac{n+3}{2}} \), i.e., \(T_5\) and \(T_6\).
name: Middle Terms for Odd n
subject: maths

text: Optical isomerism (enantiomers) is possible in:
options: Octahedral complexes with three bidentate ligands [M(AA)₃]
explanation: Optical isomers (non-superimposable mirror images) occur in [M(AA)₃] type octahedral complexes (e.g., [Co(en)₃]³⁺). Also [M(AA)₂BC]²⁺.
name: Optical Isomerism in Complexes
subject: che

text: If \( \omega \) is a complex cube root of unity, then \( \omega^{100} + \omega^{200} + \omega^{300} = \)
explanation: \( 100 = 3\times33+1,\ 200=3\times66+2,\ 300=3\times100 \). So \( \omega^1 + \omega^2 + 1 = 0+1 \)... Correction: \( \omega^{300} = (\omega^3)^{100} = 1 \), \( \omega^{100}=\omega \), \( \omega^{200}=\omega^2 \). Sum \( = 1+\omega+\omega^2+1-1 = 3\cdot1 \) for this pattern gives 3.
name: Omega Powers Sum
subject: maths

text: An object is placed at 30 cm from a convex lens of focal length 10 cm. The image distance is:
explanation: \( \frac{1}{v}-\frac{1}{-30} = \frac{1}{10} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{v} = \frac{1}{10}-\frac{1}{30} = \frac{3-1}{30} = \frac{2}{30} \Rightarrow v = 15\,\text{cm} \).
name: Lens Image Distance Calculation
subject: phy

text: Power is defined as:
options: Work done per unit time: \( P = W/t = \vec{F}\cdot\vec{v} \)
explanation: Power \( P = \frac{dW}{dt} = \vec{F}\cdot\vec{v} \). SI unit: Watt (W) = J/s.
name: Definition of Power
subject: phy

text: The back-EMF in an electric motor is:
options: In the same direction as applied EMF
explanation: Back-EMF: EMF induced by rotating armature opposing applied EMF (Lenz's law). \( I = (V - \varepsilon_{back})/R \). At full speed, back-EMF approaches applied EMF.
name: Back-EMF in Motor
subject: phy

text: For two circles with centers \( d \) apart and radii \( r_1, r_2 \), they are externally tangent when:
explanation: External tangency: \( d = r_1 + r_2 \). Internal tangency: \( d = |r_1 - r_2| \).
name: External Tangency
subject: maths

text: For circle \( x^2+y^2+2gx+2fy+c=0 \), the radius is:
explanation: Radius \( r = \sqrt{g^2+f^2-c} \). Requires \( g^2+f^2-c > 0 \) for real circle.
name: Radius from General Form
subject: maths

explanation: \( f(f(x)) = \frac{\frac{x+1}{x-1}+1}{\frac{x+1}{x-1}-1} = \frac{2x}{2} = x \). So \(f\) is its own inverse.
name: Function Composed with Itself
subject: maths

text: Bleaching powder contains:
explanation: Bleaching powder: Ca(OCl)Cl (mixed salt of Ca²⁺ with OCl⁻ and Cl⁻). Formula: CaOCl₂ or Ca(OCl)Cl. Made by Cl₂ + Ca(OH)₂.
name: Bleaching Powder
subject: che

explanation: Special formula: \( \int e^x[f(x)+f'(x)]dx = e^xf(x)+C \). Very useful shortcut.
name: eˣ[f+f'] Integration
subject: maths

text: The mean of squared deviations from mean is called:
explanation: Variance = mean of squared deviations from mean. SD is its square root.
name: Variance Definition
subject: maths

text: If \( A \) is a \( 3 \times 3 \) matrix, then \( |kA| = \)
explanation: For an \(n \times n\) matrix: \( |kA| = k^n|A| \). Here \(n=3\), so \(|kA| = k^3|A|\).
name: Scalar Multiple Determinant
subject: maths

text: The equation of a line with slope \(m\) and y-intercept \(c\) is:
explanation: Slope-intercept form: \( y = mx + c \)
name: Slope-Intercept Form
subject: maths

text: \( \int \frac{f'(x)}{f(x)}\,dx = \)
explanation: If numerator is derivative of denominator: \( \int \frac{f'(x)}{f(x)}dx = \ln|f(x)|+C \)
name: Log Form Integration
subject: maths

text: The photoelectric effect is explained by:
explanation: Einstein explained photoelectric effect using photon concept: \( KE = h\nu - h\nu_0 \) (photon energy - work function).
name: Photoelectric Effect
subject: che

text: Resonance in a forced oscillation occurs when:
explanation: Resonance: when driving frequency \( \omega_d = \omega_0 \) (natural frequency). Amplitude becomes maximum. Can cause structural failure.
name: Resonance Condition
subject: phy

text: If \( \sin\theta = \cos\theta \), then the general solution is:
explanation: \( \sin\theta = \cos\theta \Rightarrow \tan\theta = 1 \Rightarrow \theta = n\pi + \frac{\pi}{4} \)
name: sinθ = cosθ
subject: maths

text: \( \cosh x = \)
explanation: Definition: \( \cosh x = \frac{e^x + e^{-x}}{2} \)
name: Definition of cosh
subject: maths

text: If \( \alpha, \beta, \gamma \) are roots of \( x^3 + px + q = 0 \), then \( \alpha^2 + \beta^2 + \gamma^2 = \)
explanation: \( \alpha^2+\beta^2+\gamma^2 = (\alpha+\beta+\gamma)^2 - 2(\alpha\beta+\beta\gamma+\gamma\alpha) = 0 - 2p = -2p \)
name: Sum of Squares of Roots
subject: maths

text: The enthalpy of formation of water (\(\Delta H^\circ_f\)) at 25°C is approximately:
explanation: \( \text{H}_2(g) + \frac{1}{2}\text{O}_2(g) \to \text{H}_2\text{O}(l) \), \(\Delta H^\circ_f = -286\) kJ/mol (exothermic).
name: Enthalpy of Formation of Water
subject: che

text: If three points \( A, B, C \) are collinear, then area of \( \triangle ABC = \)
explanation: Collinear points don't form a triangle; the area formula gives 0.
name: Collinear Points
subject: maths

text: n-type semiconductors are formed by doping with:
explanation: n-type: doped with Group 15 element (5 valence electrons) → extra electron → electron-rich (negative carriers).
name: n-type Semiconductor
subject: che

text: The moment of inertia of a solid sphere about its diameter is:
explanation: Solid sphere (diameter): \( I = \frac{2}{5}MR^2 \). Hollow sphere: \( \frac{2}{3}MR^2 \). Solid disc: \( \frac{1}{2}MR^2 \).
name: MOI of Solid Sphere
subject: phy

text: If \( \alpha, \beta \) are roots of \( 2x^2 - 5x + 3 = 0 \), then \( \alpha + \beta = \)
explanation: Sum of roots \( = -\frac{b}{a} = -\frac{-5}{2} = \frac{5}{2} \)
name: Sum of Roots
subject: maths

text: The elastic potential energy stored per unit volume (energy density) in a stretched wire is:
explanation: Energy density \( = \frac{1}{2}\times\text{stress}\times\text{strain} = \frac{1}{2}Y(\text{strain})^2 \). Both expressions are equivalent.
name: Elastic Energy Density
subject: phy

text: The number of handshakes when 10 people each shake hands with every other is:
explanation: Each handshake involves 2 people: \( ^{10}C_2 = 45 \)
name: Handshakes Problem
subject: maths

text: The approximate value formula using differentials is:
explanation: Linear approximation: \( f(x+\Delta x) \approx f(x) + f'(x)\cdot\Delta x \)
name: Linear Approximation
subject: maths

text: The formula for the length of median \( m_a \) is:
explanation: Median to side \(a\): \( m_a = \frac{1}{2}\sqrt{2b^2+2c^2-a^2} \)
name: Length of Median
subject: maths

text: The scalar triple product \( [\vec{a}\,\vec{b}\,\vec{c}] = \vec{a}\cdot(\vec{b}\times\vec{c}) \) gives:
explanation: \( |[\vec{a}\,\vec{b}\,\vec{c}]| \) = volume of parallelepiped formed by \(\vec{a}, \vec{b}, \vec{c}\).
name: Scalar Triple Product
subject: maths

text: Pauli's exclusion principle states that no two electrons in an atom can have:
explanation: Pauli's Exclusion Principle: No two electrons in an atom can have the same set of all four quantum numbers.
name: Pauli Exclusion Principle
subject: che

text: The units of rate constant \(k\) for a second-order reaction are:
explanation: Units of \(k\): \( [k] = \frac{[\text{rate}]}{[\text{conc}]^n} = \frac{\text{mol L}^{-1}\text{s}^{-1}}{(\text{mol L}^{-1})^2} = \text{L mol}^{-1}\text{s}^{-1} \) for 2nd order.
name: Units of k (2nd Order)
subject: che

text: The curve \( y = f(x) \) is concave upward when:
explanation: Concave upward (cup shape) when \( f''(x) > 0 \). Concave downward when \( f''(x) < 0 \).
name: Concavity
subject: maths

text: The order of \( \frac{d^2y}{dx^2} + 3\frac{dy}{dx} + 2y = 0 \) is:
explanation: Order = highest derivative present. Here \( \frac{d^2y}{dx^2} \) is the highest → order = 2.
name: Order of DE
subject: maths

text: PAN (peroxyacetyl nitrate) is a component of:
explanation: PAN (CH₃CO–O–O–NO₂): formed in photochemical smog. Powerful lachrymator (causes eye tearing), damages plants, harms respiratory system.
name: PAN in Smog
subject: che

text: The normal form of a line is \( x\cos\alpha + y\sin\alpha = p \). Here \(p\) represents:
explanation: In normal form, \(p\) is the perpendicular distance from the origin to the line, always positive.
name: Normal Form
subject: maths

text: The property \( \int_a^b f(x)\,dx = \int_a^b f(a+b-x)\,dx \) is used to evaluate:
options: Integrals that are difficult to solve directly
explanation: This \(x \to a+b-x\) substitution simplifies integrals like \( \int_0^{\pi} \frac{x\sin x}{1+\cos^2 x}dx \).
name: King's Property
subject: maths

text: In SN2 reaction, the nucleophile attacks:
options: Same side as the leaving group
Opposite side (backside attack) to the leaving group
explanation: SN2: bimolecular, one-step, backside attack (180°). Causes Walden inversion (inversion of configuration at chiral centre).
name: SN2 Mechanism
subject: che

text: For a compound to be aromatic by Hückel's rule, it must have:
options: (4n+2) π electrons in a planar cyclic conjugated system
An even number of π electrons
explanation: Hückel's rule: aromatic if planar, cyclic, fully conjugated, with (4n+2) π electrons (n = 0,1,2...). Benzene: n=1, 6π electrons.
name: Hückel's Rule
subject: che

text: \( \lim_{x \to 0} \frac{e^x - 1}{x} = \)
explanation: Standard exponential limit: \( \lim_{x\to 0}\frac{e^x-1}{x} = 1 \)
name: Exponential Limit
subject: maths

text: If \( \vec{a} = 2\hat{i}+3\hat{j}+\hat{k} \) and \( \vec{b} = \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2\hat{k} \), then \( \vec{a}\cdot\vec{b} = \)
name: Dot Product Calculation
subject: maths

text: The area between curve \( y=f(x) \) and x-axis from \(a\) to \(b\) is:
explanation: Area \( = \int_a^b |f(x)|\,dx \). Use \( |f(x)| \) to handle regions below x-axis.
name: Area Under Curve
subject: maths

text: A square matrix \( A \) is singular if:
explanation: A matrix is singular if and only if its determinant is zero.
name: Singular Matrix
subject: maths

text: The oxoacids of phosphorus: phosphoric acid (H₃PO₄) has how many P–OH groups (proton donating)?
explanation: H₃PO₄ (orthophosphoric acid): 3 P–OH groups → tribasic acid. H₃PO₃: 2 P–OH → dibasic. H₃PO₂: 1 P–OH → monobasic.
name: Basicity of H₃PO₄
subject: che

text: The number of significant figures in 0.00450 is:
explanation: Leading zeros are not significant. In 0.00450: 4, 5, 0 (trailing zero after decimal) = 3 significant figures.
name: Significant Figures
subject: phy

text: The standard equation of an ellipse with semi-major axis \(a\) along x-axis is:
explanation: Ellipse: \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}+\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \) with \(a>b>0\). Foci at \( (\pm c,0) \) where \( c^2=a^2-b^2 \).
name: Ellipse Standard Form
subject: maths

text: Number of ways to choose 3 students from a group of 8 is:
name: Selection
subject: maths

text: The excess pressure inside a soap bubble of radius \(R\) is:
explanation: Soap bubble has 2 surfaces: \( \Delta P = \frac{4T}{R} \). Single liquid drop: \( \Delta P = \frac{2T}{R} \).
name: Excess Pressure in Soap Bubble
subject: phy

text: The second ionisation energy is always greater than the first because:
options: Second electron is in higher shell
Removing electron from positive ion requires more energy
explanation: After removing first electron, the cation has higher effective nuclear charge per electron → second IE > first IE always.
name: Second vs First IE
subject: che

text: Phenol gives a violet/purple colour with FeCl₃ due to formation of:
explanation: Phenol + FeCl₃ → violet/purple iron(III) phenoxide complex. Used as a test for phenols. Enols also give positive test.
name: FeCl₃ Test for Phenol
subject: che

text: The equation with roots \( \frac{1}{\alpha}, \frac{1}{\beta}, \frac{1}{\gamma} \) (where \(\alpha,\beta,\gamma\) are roots of \( x^3-2x^2+3x-4=0 \)) is:
name: Reciprocal Roots Equation
subject: maths

text: At constant pressure, the heat change equals:
explanation: \( q_p = \Delta H \) (enthalpy change). At constant volume: \( q_v = \Delta U \).
name: Heat at Constant Pressure
subject: che

text: Diffraction grating equation for principal maxima is:
explanation: Diffraction grating: \( d\sin\theta = n\lambda \), where \(d\) = grating spacing (1/N per mm), \(n\) = order of diffraction.
name: Diffraction Grating Equation
subject: phy

text: Work done by a force \(\vec{F}\) through displacement \(\vec{s}\) is:
explanation: Work \( W = \vec{F}\cdot\vec{s} = Fs\cos\theta \), where \(\theta\) is angle between \(\vec{F}\) and \(\vec{s}\).
name: Work Done by Force
subject: phy

text: Principal solution of \( \sin\theta = \frac{1}{2} \) in \( [0, 2\pi] \) are:
explanation: \( \sin\theta = \frac{1}{2} \) in \([0,2\pi]\): \( \theta = \frac{\pi}{6} \) (1st quad) and \( \pi - \frac{\pi}{6} = \frac{5\pi}{6} \) (2nd quad).
name: Principal Solutions
subject: maths

text: The focus of parabola \( y^2 = 4ax \) is:
explanation: For \( y^2 = 4ax \): vertex at origin, focus at \((a,0)\), directrix \(x=-a\).
name: Focus of Parabola
subject: maths

text: The general electronic configuration of transition elements (3d series) is:
explanation: 3d transition series: [Ar] 3d¹⁻¹⁰ 4s¹⁻². Exceptions: Cr ([Ar]3d⁵4s¹) and Cu ([Ar]3d¹⁰4s¹) due to extra stability of half-filled/fully-filled d.
name: Electronic Config of Transition Elements
subject: che

text: The angular velocity \(\omega\) is related to linear velocity \(v\) and radius \(r\) by:
explanation: \( v = r\omega \). Tangential velocity of a point at radius \(r\) from axis rotating at angular velocity \(\omega\).
name: v and ω Relation
subject: phy

text: For the Haber process \( \text{N}_2 + 3\text{H}_2 \rightleftharpoons 2\text{NH}_3 \), increasing pressure:
options: Shifts equilibrium to left (more N₂ and H₂)
explanation: Reactants: 4 mol gas; Products: 2 mol gas. Increasing pressure → shift to side with fewer moles of gas → right (more NH₃).
name: Haber Process and Pressure
subject: che

text: A fair die is rolled. The probability of getting a number \( > 4 \) is:
name: Simple Probability - Die
subject: maths

text: A reaction is spontaneous at all temperatures when:
explanation: \( \Delta G = \Delta H - T\Delta S \). When \(\Delta H < 0\) and \(\Delta S > 0\): \(\Delta G < 0\) always (at all T).
name: Spontaneous at All T
subject: che

text: In \( \frac{3x+5}{(x-1)(x+2)} = \frac{A}{x-1} + \frac{B}{x+2} \), the value of \( A \) is:
explanation: Set \(x=1\): \( 3+5 = A(3) \Rightarrow A = \frac{8}{3} \)
name: Finding Constant A
subject: maths

text: The domain of \( f(x) = \log(x^2 - x - 6) \) is:
explanation: Need \( x^2-x-6 > 0 \Rightarrow (x-3)(x+2) > 0 \Rightarrow x > 3 \) or \( x < -2 \)
name: Domain of Logarithm
subject: maths

text: A p-n junction diode conducts in:
options: Forward bias (forward current flows when V > knee voltage ~0.7V for Si)
explanation: Forward bias: p-side to + terminal. Reduces barrier → significant current flows. Reverse bias: increases barrier → only tiny leakage current.
name: Diode Conduction
subject: phy

text: \( P(A \cup B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A \cap B) \). If \( P(A)=0.3, P(B)=0.4, P(A \cap B)=0.1 \), then \( P(A \cup B) = \)
name: Addition Theorem
subject: maths

text: The wave equation \( \frac{\partial^2 y}{\partial t^2} = v^2 \frac{\partial^2 y}{\partial x^2} \) is satisfied by:
options: Any wave function of form \( y = f(x \pm vt) \)
explanation: The general solution is \( y = f(x-vt) + g(x+vt) \) — any shape travelling in \(+x\) or \(-x\) direction.
name: Wave Equation
subject: phy

text: For an ideal gas, \( C_p - C_v = \)
explanation: Meyer's relation: \( C_p - C_v = R \) for one mole of ideal gas. \(C_p > C_v\) because extra work done in constant pressure expansion.
name: Meyer's Relation
subject: phy

text: The RMS value of an AC voltage \( V = V_0\sin(\omega t) \) is:
explanation: RMS value: \( V_{rms} = V_0/\sqrt{2} \). For Indian household supply: \( V_{rms}=220\,\text{V} \Rightarrow V_0\approx 311\,\text{V} \).
name: RMS Voltage
subject: phy

text: The formula to calculate molar mass from freezing point depression is:
explanation: \( M_2 = \frac{K_f \times w_2 \times 1000}{\Delta T_f \times w_1} \), where \(w_1\) = mass of solvent (g), \(w_2\) = mass of solute (g).
name: Molar Mass from FP Depression
subject: che

options: Fission of U-235 by slow (thermal) neutron
explanation: This is the fission of U-235 by thermal neutron producing Ba-141, Kr-92, 3 neutrons, and ~200 MeV energy. These 3 neutrons can trigger more fissions (chain reaction).
name: U-235 Fission Reaction
subject: phy

text: In a medium with refractive index \(n\), the speed of EM wave is:
explanation: Speed in medium: \( v = c/n \), where \( n = \sqrt{\mu_r\epsilon_r} \). Light slows down in denser media (higher \(n\)).
name: Speed of EM Wave in Medium
subject: phy

text: \( \lim_{x \to 0} \frac{\tan x}{x} = \)
explanation: \( \lim_{x\to 0}\frac{\tan x}{x} = \lim_{x\to 0}\frac{\sin x}{x}\cdot\frac{1}{\cos x} = 1\cdot1 = 1 \)
name: tan x / x Limit
subject: maths

text: If \( \alpha, \beta \) are roots of \( x^2 - 3x + 2 = 0 \), then \( \frac{1}{\alpha} + \frac{1}{\beta} = \)
explanation: \( \frac{1}{\alpha}+\frac{1}{\beta} = \frac{\alpha+\beta}{\alpha\beta} = \frac{3}{2} \)
name: Sum of Reciprocals
subject: maths

text: Langmuir adsorption isotherm equation is:
explanation: Langmuir isotherm: \( \frac{x}{m} = \frac{abP}{1+bP} \). Linear form: \( \frac{P}{x/m} = \frac{1}{ab}+\frac{P}{a} \).
name: Langmuir Isotherm
subject: che

text: The rate of reaction is defined as:
options: Increase in concentration of products per unit time
explanation: Rate \( = -\frac{d[A]}{dt} = +\frac{d[P]}{dt} \). Negative sign for reactants (decreasing), positive for products.
name: Rate of Reaction
subject: che

text: The percentage composition of oxygen in \( \text{H}_2\text{O} \) is:
name: Percentage Composition
subject: che

text: The magnetic dipole moment of a bar magnet is \( M = m \times 2l \). The field on its axial line is:
explanation: Axial field: \( B = \frac{\mu_0}{4\pi}\frac{2M}{r^3} \). Equatorial: \( B = \frac{\mu_0}{4\pi}\frac{M}{r^3} \). Axial:Equatorial = 2:1 (same as electric dipole).
name: Bar Magnet Axial Field
subject: phy

text: The equation of x-axis is:
explanation: The x-axis is the line where \( y = 0 \) for all values of \(x\).
name: Equation of Axes
subject: maths

text: The range of the data set \( \{3, 7, 2, 9, 5\} \) is:
explanation: Range \( = \max - \min = 9 - 2 = 7 \)
name: Range
subject: maths

text: The general solution of \( \sin^2\theta = \sin^2\alpha \) is:
explanation: \( \sin^2\theta = \sin^2\alpha \Rightarrow \cos 2\theta = \cos 2\alpha \Rightarrow 2\theta = 2n\pi \pm 2\alpha \Rightarrow \theta = n\pi \pm \alpha \)
name: sin²θ = sin²α
subject: maths

text: Dehydration of alcohols to alkenes requires:
options: Conc. H₂SO₄ at ~170°C (excess acid, high temperature)
explanation: Dehydration (elimination): alcohol + conc. H₂SO₄ at 170°C → alkene (excess H₂SO₄). At 140°C with 2 mol alcohol → ether (Williamson synthesis).
name: Dehydration of Alcohols
subject: che

text: In \( \frac{1}{x^2-1} = \frac{A}{x-1} + \frac{B}{x+1} \), then \( A + B = \)
name: Sum of Constants
subject: maths

text: The EMF of a cell is defined as work done per unit charge to move charge:
options: Through complete circuit (including internal resistance): EMF = W/q
explanation: EMF: work done per unit positive charge through entire circuit (including inside cell) = energy per unit charge supplied by cell. Units: Volt.
name: Definition of EMF
subject: phy

text: The sum \( 1^2 + 2^2 + \cdots + n^2 = \)
explanation: \( \sum_{k=1}^n k^2 = \frac{n(n+1)(2n+1)}{6} \). Standard formula verified by induction.
name: Sum of Squares
subject: maths

text: The value of \( \sin^2\theta + \cos^2\theta = \)
explanation: Pythagorean identity: \( \sin^2\theta + \cos^2\theta = 1 \) for all \(\theta\).
name: Pythagorean Identity
subject: maths

text: For proving \( P(n): 2^n > n \) for all \( n \geq 1 \), the base case is verified at \( n = \)
explanation: At \( n=1 \): \( 2^1 = 2 > 1 \). Base case holds.
name: Base Case Verification
subject: maths

text: The internal resistance \(r\) of a battery can be found using:
explanation: Terminal voltage: \( V = E - Ir \). When external \(R\) is connected: \( I = E/(R+r) \) and \( V = IR = E-Ir \).
name: Internal Resistance
subject: phy

text: The latus rectum of parabola \( y^2=4ax \) has length:
explanation: Length of latus rectum of \( y^2=4ax \) is \( 4a \) (chord through focus perpendicular to axis).
name: Latus Rectum of Parabola
subject: maths

text: The magnetic moment formula for transition metal ions is:
explanation: Magnetic moment: \( \mu = \sqrt{n(n+2)} \) Bohr Magnetons (BM), where n = number of unpaired electrons.
name: Magnetic Moment Formula
subject: che

text: The volume occupied by 1 mole of an ideal gas at STP (0°C, 1 atm) is:
explanation: Molar volume of ideal gas at STP = 22.4 L/mol (standard condition: 0°C, 1 atm).
name: Molar Volume at STP
subject: che

text: Area of circle \( x^2+y^2=r^2 \) using integration is:
explanation: Area \( = 4\int_0^r \sqrt{r^2-x^2}\,dx = \pi r^2 \). Verified by standard integration.
name: Area of Circle
subject: maths

text: The period of \( f(x) = \tan x \) is:
explanation: \( \tan(x+\pi) = \tan x \) for all \(x\). The period is \(\pi\).
name: Period of Tangent
subject: maths

text: In MO theory, bond order \( = \)
explanation: MO Bond Order \( = \frac{N_b - N_a}{2} \), where \(N_b\) = bonding electrons, \(N_a\) = antibonding electrons.
name: MO Bond Order Formula
subject: che

text: At a local maximum, the second derivative satisfies:
explanation: Second Derivative Test: local max iff \( f'(x)=0 \) and \( f''(x)<0 \).
name: Second Derivative Test - Max
subject: maths

text: The truth table of an OR gate: output is 1 when:
explanation: OR gate: output = 1 if any input = 1. Output = 0 only when ALL inputs = 0. Boolean: \( Y = A+B \) (logical sum).
name: OR Gate Truth Table
subject: phy

text: The loss of energy when two capacitors are connected is due to:
options: Heat generated in connecting wire (even if R→0)
explanation: Energy is always lost as heat when capacitors share charge, even with ideal conductors. This is a fundamental result of charge redistribution.
name: Energy Loss in Capacitor Connection
subject: phy

text: The elevation of boiling point formula is:
explanation: \( \Delta T_b = K_b \times m \), where \(K_b\) = ebullioscopic constant, \(m\) = molality.
name: Boiling Point Elevation
subject: che

text: The argument of \( z = -1 - i \) is:
explanation: \( z = -1-i \) lies in 3rd quadrant. \( \arg(z) = -(\pi - \frac{\pi}{4}) = -\frac{3\pi}{4} \)
name: Argument of Complex Number
subject: maths

text: \( \frac{d}{dx}(\sin x) = \)
explanation: \( \frac{d}{dx}(\sin x) = \cos x \)
name: Derivative of sin
subject: maths

text: The pseudo force (fictitious force) appears in:
explanation: Pseudo force: \( \vec{F}_{pseudo} = -m\vec{a}_{frame} \). Appears in non-inertial frames to explain motion using Newton's laws.
name: Pseudo Force
subject: phy

text: The banking of roads is done to provide:
options: Centripetal force without depending on friction
explanation: Banking: \( \tan\theta = \frac{v^2}{rg} \). Normal force component provides centripetal force without relying on friction. Optimal speed: \( v = \sqrt{rg\tan\theta} \).
name: Banking of Roads
subject: phy

text: If \( [\vec{a}\,\vec{b}\,\vec{c}] = 0 \), the vectors are:
explanation: Zero scalar triple product means the three vectors are coplanar (lie in the same plane).
name: Coplanar Vectors
subject: maths

text: The de Broglie wavelength of an electron accelerated through potential \(V\) is:
explanation: \( eV = \frac{1}{2}mv^2 = p^2/2m \Rightarrow p = \sqrt{2meV} \). So \( \lambda = h/\sqrt{2meV} \). For 100V: \(\lambda \approx 1.2\) Å.
name: de Broglie Wavelength of Electron
subject: phy

text: In Lloyd's mirror experiment, the fringe pattern is shifted by half a fringe width because:
options: One beam undergoes phase change of \(\pi\) on reflection (causing a dark fringe at centre)
explanation: Lloyd's mirror: one source is real, one is virtual (image in mirror). Reflection at denser surface → phase change of \(\pi\) → dark central fringe (unlike YDSE with bright centre).
name: Lloyd's Mirror
subject: phy

text: The number of 4th roots of unity is:
explanation: The equation \( z^4 = 1 \) has exactly 4 roots: \( 1, -1, i, -i \)
name: Roots of Unity Count
subject: maths

text: The equilibrium constant \(K\) is related to \(E^\circ\) by:
explanation: At 25°C: \( \log K = \frac{nE^\circ}{0.0592} \) (from \( \Delta G^\circ = -nFE^\circ = -RT\ln K \)).
name: K from E°
subject: che

text: If \( f(x) = \cos(x^2) \), then \( f'(x) = \)
explanation: Chain rule: \( \frac{d}{dx}\cos(x^2) = -\sin(x^2)\cdot 2x = -2x\sin(x^2) \)
name: Chain Rule Application
subject: maths

text: The monomer units of proteins are:
explanation: Proteins: polymers of α-amino acids linked by peptide bonds (–CO–NH–). 20 standard amino acids form all proteins.
name: Protein Monomers
subject: che

text: Glyptal is a polyester used as paint/varnish, formed from:
explanation: Glyptal (alkyd resin): glycerol (triol) + phthalic anhydride → cross-linked polyester. Used in paints, lacquers.
name: Glyptal Polyester
subject: che

text: The ILATE rule for integration by parts orders: I=Inverse trig, L=Log, A=Algebraic, T=Trig, E=Exponential. The function to choose as \(u\) is:
options: The one that comes later in ILATE
The one that comes first in ILATE
explanation: In ILATE, choose \(u\) as the function that comes first: Inverse trig > Log > Algebraic > Trig > Exponential.
name: ILATE Rule
subject: maths

text: The ideal gas equation is:
explanation: Ideal Gas Law: \( PV = nRT \), where \(R = 8.314\) J/mol·K (or 0.0821 L·atm/mol·K).
name: Ideal Gas Equation
subject: che

text: \( \cos 3\theta = \)
explanation: Triple angle formula: \( \cos 3\theta = 4\cos^3\theta - 3\cos\theta \)
name: Triple Angle - cos
subject: maths

text: Carbylamine reaction (isocyanide test) is given by:
options: Primary amines (1°) to give foul-smelling isocyanide
explanation: Carbylamine test: 1° amine + CHCl₃ + KOH → isocyanide (R–NC), very unpleasant smell. Positive only for primary amines. Test for 1° amines.
name: Carbylamine Test
subject: che

text: The angle between two planes is equal to the angle between their:
explanation: Dihedral angle between planes = angle between their normal vectors.
name: Angle Between Planes
subject: maths

text: The energy stored in a capacitor is:
explanation: Energy in capacitor: \( U = \frac{1}{2}CV^2 = \frac{Q^2}{2C} = \frac{QV}{2} \). Stored as electric field energy.
name: Energy in Capacitor
subject: phy

text: \( \sin 2\theta = \)
explanation: \( \sin 2\theta = 2\sin\theta\cos\theta \). Also \( = \frac{2\tan\theta}{1+\tan^2\theta} \).
name: Double Angle - sin
subject: maths

text: In a binomial distribution \( B(n, p) \), the mean is:
explanation: Mean of Binomial Distribution \( = np \), Variance \( = npq \) where \( q = 1-p \).
name: Binomial Mean
subject: maths

text: If \( \alpha, \beta \) are roots of \( 2x^2 - 5x + 3 = 0 \), then \( \alpha \cdot \beta = \)
explanation: Product of roots \( = \frac{c}{a} = \frac{3}{2} \)
name: Product of Roots
subject: maths

text: The relationship between wave speed \(v\), frequency \(f\), and wavelength \(\lambda\) is:
explanation: Fundamental wave relation: \( v = f\lambda \). Or \( v = \omega/k \) where \(k = 2\pi/\lambda\) is wave number.
name: Wave Speed Relation
subject: phy

text: The quantity of electricity required to deposit 1 mole of Cu (2+ ion) is:
explanation: \( \text{Cu}^{2+} + 2e^- \to \text{Cu} \). 2 moles of electrons needed = 2F = \( 2 \times 96500 = 193000 \) C.
name: Charge to Deposit Cu
subject: che

text: The most probable speed \(v_p\) in Maxwell's distribution is:
explanation: Most probable speed: \( v_p = \sqrt{2RT/M} \). Average: \( \bar{v} = \sqrt{8RT/\pi M} \). RMS: \( v_{rms} = \sqrt{3RT/M} \). Order: \( v_p < \bar{v} < v_{rms} \).
name: Most Probable Speed
subject: phy

text: The angle bisectors of \( ax^2+2hxy+by^2=0 \) are given by:
explanation: Equation of angle bisectors: \( \frac{x^2-y^2}{a-b} = \frac{xy}{h} \)
name: Angle Bisectors of Pair
subject: maths

text: The number of oscillations per second is called:
explanation: Frequency \( f = 1/T \), units = Hz (hertz). Relation: \( \omega = 2\pi f \). For pendulum: \( f = \frac{1}{2\pi}\sqrt{g/l} \).
name: Frequency of Oscillation
subject: phy

text: The DE \( \frac{dy}{dx}+y\tan x = \sec x \) has integrating factor:
explanation: IF \( = e^{\int\tan x\,dx} = e^{\ln|\sec x|} = \sec x \)
name: IF Calculation
subject: maths

text: The inradius \(r\) of a triangle is:
explanation: Inradius \( r = \frac{\Delta}{s} \) where \( s = \frac{a+b+c}{2} \) is the semi-perimeter.
name: Inradius Formula
subject: maths

text: A ball is thrown vertically upward with velocity \(20\) m/s. Maximum height reached (\(g=10\) m/s²) is:
explanation: \( v^2 = u^2 - 2gH \Rightarrow 0 = 400 - 2(10)H \Rightarrow H = 20\,\text{m} \).
name: Max Height - Vertical Throw
subject: phy

text: \( \lim_{x \to \infty} \left(1+\frac{1}{x}\right)^x = \)
explanation: Definition of \(e\): \( \lim_{x\to\infty}\left(1+\frac{1}{x}\right)^x = e \approx 2.718 \)
name: Definition of e
subject: maths

text: The intercept form of a plane is:
explanation: Intercept form: \( \frac{x}{a}+\frac{y}{b}+\frac{z}{c}=1 \), where \(a,b,c\) are x,y,z intercepts.
name: Plane Intercept Form
subject: maths

text: Diagonal relationship exists between:
explanation: Diagonal relationship: Li-Mg, Be-Al, B-Si. Elements show similarity with the element diagonally below-right due to similar charge/size ratio.
name: Diagonal Relationship
subject: che

text: Gabriel phthalimide synthesis is used to prepare:
explanation: Gabriel synthesis: phthalimide + KOH → potassium phthalimide + RX → N-alkyl phthalimide + N₂H₄/H₂O → pure 1° amine. No 2°/3° contamination.
name: Gabriel Synthesis
subject: che

text: Kirchhoff's voltage law (KVL) states:
options: Current is constant around a loop
Sum of all voltages (EMFs and drops) around any closed loop = 0
Voltage is same across all elements
explanation: KVL (loop rule): \( \sum V = \sum EMF - \sum IR = 0 \). Based on conservation of energy.
name: Kirchhoff's Voltage Law
subject: phy

text: The bond angle in \( \text{H}_2\text{O} \) is approximately:
explanation: \(\text{H}_2\text{O}\): 2 bond pairs + 2 lone pairs. Lone pair repulsion reduces angle from 109.5° to ≈104.5°.
name: Bond Angle of H₂O
subject: che

text: If 1 is a root of \( x^3 + ax^2 + bx - 2 = 0 \), then:
explanation: Substituting \(x=1\): \( 1 + a + b - 2 = 0 \Rightarrow a + b = 1 \)
name: Root Substitution Condition
subject: maths

text: From a pack of 52 cards, probability of drawing a king is:
explanation: 4 kings in 52 cards: \( P = \frac{4}{52} = \frac{1}{13} \)
name: Card Probability
subject: maths

text: In an adiabatic process:
explanation: Adiabatic: thermally insulated system, \( Q = 0 \). Work done by gas comes from internal energy: \( W = -\Delta U \).
name: Adiabatic Process
subject: phy

text: The common chord of two intersecting circles lies on:
explanation: The common chord of two intersecting circles is the radical axis: \( S_1 - S_2 = 0 \).
name: Common Chord
subject: maths

text: The equation of a plane in normal form is:
explanation: Normal form of plane: \( lx+my+nz=p \), where \(l,m,n\) are direction cosines of normal and \(p>0\).
name: Plane Normal Form
subject: maths
